@@ -1017,7 +1017,7 @@
 {"id":"wa-oegrb.2.1","title":"Define versioned recorder event schema for ingress/egress mux text","description":"## Goal
Define a versioned canonical event schema for mux ingress/egress text capture, including metadata needed for replay, search filtering, and causality analysis.

## Background
Schema ambiguity will invalidate downstream indexing and replay. This bead establishes the ground-truth contract.

## Scope
- Define event types (ingress text, egress output, control markers, lifecycle markers).
- Define required metadata (pane/session/workflow correlation IDs, timestamps, sequence numbers, source).
- Define schema evolution/versioning rules and compatibility tests.

## Deliverables
- Recorder event schema spec and serialization contract.
- JSON-schema or equivalent testable schema artifact.

## Acceptance criteria
- Schema is precise enough for independent producer/consumer implementation.
- Evolution rules cover additive and breaking changes explicitly.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PinkGate","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:11.66701Z","created_by":"WildSpring","updated_at":"2026-02-12T02:11:47.023622-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-12T02:11:47.023622-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented alongside ft-oegrb.2.1 — same deliverables.","labels":["capture","flight-recorder","schema"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.2.1","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:11.66701Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"wa-oegrb.2.2","title":"Implement ingress tap points for all mux-bound text/action injections","description":"## Goal
Implement ingress capture tap points (text/actions entering mux/panes) with minimal overhead and explicit source attribution.

## Background
Ingress visibility is required for full forensic replay and for understanding operator/agent actions leading to outcomes.

## Scope
- Identify ingress boundaries and hook locations.
- Capture payload + metadata in canonical schema.
- Ensure capture path does not break policy gating behavior.

## Deliverables
- Ingress capture implementation plan + instrumentation tests.
- Validation that policy decisions and ingress logs remain consistent.

## Acceptance criteria
- Ingress events are captured for all supported injection paths.
- Overhead budget for ingress capture is documented and met in benchmarks.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:11.699328Z","created_by":"WildSpring","updated_at":"2026-02-12T08:09:52.327488Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T08:09:38.47922Z","close_reason":"Reconciled duplicate: ingress tap implementation and tests already landed","labels":["capture","ingress","mux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.2.2","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:11.699328Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.2.2","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:11.864528Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"}],"comments":[{"id":11,"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.2.2","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Reconciled from duplicate ft-oegrb.2.2 completion: ingress tap integration already landed in PolicyGatedInjector () with integration coverage in  tests and related ingress tap tests. Outcome coverage includes allowed/denied/requires-approval/error paths with recorder metadata wiring.","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:09:38Z"},{"id":12,"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.2.2","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Correction: ingress tap implementation references are crates/frankenterm-core/src/policy.rs and crates/frankenterm-core/src/recording.rs. The prior comment omitted these paths due shell quoting, but closure rationale is unchanged.","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:09:52Z"}]}
 {"id":"wa-oegrb.2.3","title":"Implement egress tap points with contiguous segment + explicit gap semantics","description":"## Goal
Implement egress capture tap points (pane/mux output leaving runtime paths) with contiguous segment semantics and explicit gap markers.

## Background
Egress capture is the primary data source for replay and search; it must preserve ordering and discontinuity semantics.

## Scope
- Hook egress at the authoritative output pipeline.
- Emit explicit discontinuity markers where continuity cannot be guaranteed.
- Preserve compatibility with existing ingest/pattern detection semantics.

## Deliverables
- Egress capture instrumentation with segment/gap semantics.
- Regression tests for continuity/gap behavior.

## Acceptance criteria
- Egress events are complete for observed panes.
- Gap semantics are explicit and machine-detectable.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:11.731497Z","created_by":"WildSpring","updated_at":"2026-02-12T08:10:50.66849Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T08:10:50.668477Z","close_reason":"Reconciled duplicate: egress tap + explicit gap semantics already implemented and integration-tested","labels":["capture","egress","ingest"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.2.3","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:11.731497Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.2.3","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:11.894294Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"}],"comments":[{"id":13,"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.2.3","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Reconciled from existing implementation already present in mainline code: egress tap surface is wired in crates/frankenterm-core/src/tailer.rs and event model lives in crates/frankenterm-core/src/recording.rs. Verified with targeted test run: CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-cargo-orangebarn cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test egress_tap_integration -- --nocapture (7/7 passing), covering delta capture, gap capture, overflow gap emission, multi-pane behavior, and sequence monotonicity.","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:10:50Z"}]}
-{"id":"wa-oegrb.2.4","title":"Define deterministic sequence + correlation model for replayable causality","description":"## Goal
Define deterministic sequence and correlation rules across ingress/egress streams so replay/search can reconstruct causality.

## Background
Raw timestamps alone are insufficient under concurrency. We need deterministic ordering semantics and correlation keys.

## Scope
- Define sequence assignment rules per pane and global merge strategy.
- Define correlation metadata (pane UUID/session/workflow/action linkage).
- Define clock-skew and race-handling behavior.

## Deliverables
- Ordering/correlation specification.
- Determinism and replay correctness test plan.

## Acceptance criteria
- Ordering rules are unambiguous and test-assertable.
- Replay results are stable across repeated runs on same input.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"OrangeBarn","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:11.764919Z","created_by":"WildSpring","updated_at":"2026-02-12T08:11:20.008527Z","labels":["capture","ordering","replay"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.2.4","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:11.764919Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.2.4","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:11.925809Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.2.4","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:11.955931Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"}],"comments":[{"id":14,"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.2.4","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Claimed by OrangeBarn. Starting deterministic sequence/correlation model spec with explicit per-pane sequence rules, cross-stream merge tie-breakers, causal linkage contract, and replay-stability test plan.","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:11:20Z"}]}
+{"id":"wa-oegrb.2.4","title":"Define deterministic sequence + correlation model for replayable causality","description":"## Goal
Define deterministic sequence and correlation rules across ingress/egress streams so replay/search can reconstruct causality.

## Background
Raw timestamps alone are insufficient under concurrency. We need deterministic ordering semantics and correlation keys.

## Scope
- Define sequence assignment rules per pane and global merge strategy.
- Define correlation metadata (pane UUID/session/workflow/action linkage).
- Define clock-skew and race-handling behavior.

## Deliverables
- Ordering/correlation specification.
- Determinism and replay correctness test plan.

## Acceptance criteria
- Ordering rules are unambiguous and test-assertable.
- Replay results are stable across repeated runs on same input.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"OrangeBarn","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:11.764919Z","created_by":"WildSpring","updated_at":"2026-02-12T08:12:29.422169Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T08:12:29.422155Z","close_reason":"Deterministic sequence + correlation model documented with replay-stable merge and test requirements","labels":["capture","ordering","replay"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.2.4","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:11.764919Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.2.4","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:11.925809Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.2.4","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:11.955931Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"}],"comments":[{"id":14,"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.2.4","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Claimed by OrangeBarn. Starting deterministic sequence/correlation model spec with explicit per-pane sequence rules, cross-stream merge tie-breakers, causal linkage contract, and replay-stability test plan.","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:11:20Z"},{"id":15,"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.2.4","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Delivered deterministic ordering/correlation contract at docs/flight-recorder/sequence-correlation-model.md and linked from docs/flight-recorder/recorder-event-schema.md. Defines sequence domains, total merge key, deterministic event_id strategy, causality linkage rules, seq_discontinuity handling, clock-anomaly semantics, and replay determinism validation plan.","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:12:29Z"}]}
 {"id":"wa-oegrb.2.5","title":"Define capture-stage redaction policy and deterministic implementation boundaries","description":"## Goal
Specify and implement capture-stage redaction boundaries so sensitive tokens are protected without destroying search/replay utility.

## Background
A full recorder introduces privacy/security risk. Redaction policy must be explicit early, not bolted on later.

## Scope
- Define sensitive-pattern classes and redaction modes.
- Define where redaction is applied (before persist, before index, before query response).
- Define auditability for redaction actions.

## Deliverables
- Redaction policy and implementation requirements.
- Tests for deterministic redaction behavior and false-positive guardrails.

## Acceptance criteria
- Sensitive fields are consistently protected across storage/index/API layers.
- Redaction behavior is measurable and tunable.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"OrangeBarn","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:11.797785Z","created_by":"WildSpring","updated_at":"2026-02-12T08:08:47.317326Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T08:08:47.317313Z","close_reason":"Capture-stage redaction policy and deterministic implementation boundaries documented with downstream-ready requirements","labels":["capture","privacy","redaction","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.2.5","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:11.797785Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.2.5","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:11.987531Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"}],"comments":[{"id":9,"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.2.5","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Claimed by OrangeBarn. Starting deterministic capture-stage redaction policy design with explicit pre-persist/pre-index/pre-response boundaries, auditability requirements, and concrete test matrix mapped to downstream wa-oegrb.6.5/7.6/8.3.","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:07:05Z"},{"id":10,"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.2.5","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Delivered capture redaction policy contract at docs/flight-recorder/capture-redaction-policy.md and linked from docs/flight-recorder/recorder-event-schema.md. Policy defines deterministic pre-persist/pre-index/pre-response boundaries, required redaction metadata schema, implementation ownership by code surface, configuration/audit requirements, and full validation matrix for downstream wa-oegrb.6.5/7.6/8.3.","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:08:47Z"}]}
 {"id":"wa-oegrb.2.6","title":"Specify and validate capture backpressure/overflow behavior under swarm load","description":"## Goal
Define capture-path backpressure/failure semantics and verify behavior under overload so recorder does not destabilize core watcher operations.

## Background
Recorder capture must fail safely under stress; silent degradation is unacceptable.

## Scope
- Define queueing and overflow policy (drop/slow/spool) with rationale.
- Define telemetry and alerting conditions.
- Validate with overload and fault injection tests.

## Deliverables
- Backpressure policy doc and implementation plan.
- Overload test scenarios with expected outcomes.

## Acceptance criteria
- Overload behavior is deterministic and observable.
- Core watcher stability is preserved under recorder stress.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:11.831967Z","created_by":"WildSpring","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:12.048598Z","labels":["backpressure","capture","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.2.6","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:11.831967Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.2.6","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:12.018117Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.2.6","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:12.048557Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"wa-oegrb.3","title":"Storage track: append-only writer path + backend abstraction + FrankenSQLite integration","description":"## Track objective
Build the append-only persistence path for flight-recorder events, including backend abstraction, high-throughput writer path, and FrankenSQLite integration path with safe fallback.

## Why this track exists
Logging quality is bounded by write-path reliability and throughput. This track ensures durability under load while preserving rollout safety.

## Deliverables
- Storage abstraction for recorder log backends
- Baseline backend (for immediate bring-up)
- FrankenSQLite backend plan/integration path
- Crash recovery/replay and corruption handling strategy

## Acceptance criteria
- Sustained append throughput targets are met under concurrent writer load.
- Failure modes are explicit and exercised in automated tests.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:02:58.285668Z","created_by":"WildSpring","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:09:16.717242Z","labels":["append-only","flight-recorder","frankensqlite","storage"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:02:58.285668Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:09:16.678028Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:09:16.717172Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
